title: galium baldense var. tendae
intro: Galium baldense var. tendae, also known as Alpine Bedstraw, is a subspecies of the Galium genus, a type of flowering plant in the madder family. Traditionally, Alpine Bedstraw has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral effects, which help to soothe wounds, reduce swelling, and fight off infections. The health benefits of Alpine Bedstraw include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and promote digestive health, making it a popular choice for herbal remedies. Alpine Bedstraw can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties and provide a more comprehensive treatment.
